_comment_nist_fields: Fields below follow IR 8278Ar1 Section 3.2 OLIR template
focal_document_element_description: Skill to articulate a needs statement/requirement and integrate new and emerging collection capabilities, accesses and/or processes into collection operations.
reference_document_element_description: Skill in conducting data consumer discovery, translating consumer data needs into data product requirements with defined quality, freshness, and access standards.
comments: DCWF ksa superset of WIDAI DQ domain. DQ-S-007 is the strongest semantic match. Secondary: DQ-S-006 (DQ, cosine: 0.557), TF-A-003 (TF, cosine: 0.519).
_comment_widai_extensions: Fields below are WIDAI extensions per ADR-014

primary_method.name: Cross-Encoder STS
primary_method.description: Semantic Textual Similarity via cross-encoder regression trained on STS-Benchmark.
primary_method.significance: STS 6.9/10 (raw 0.688). Shared vocabulary: [needs, skill]. DCWF-only terms [accesses, articulate, capabilities, collection, emerging, integrate] vs WIDAI-only terms [access, conducting, consumer, data, defined, discovery] define the semantic distance between the descriptions.

bertscore.significance: BERTScore F1=0.877 (P=0.873, R=0.881). Balanced P/R — the 19-word DCWF element and 21-word WIDAI KSA cover each other's token-level semantic content symmetrically.

nli_cross_encoder.significance: NLI: neutral-dominant (99.8%). "Skill to articulate a needs statement/requirement and integrate new and emerging" and "Skill in conducting data consumer discovery, translating consumer data needs int" are related but neither implies nor contradicts the other. Standard result for cross-framework mapping where two independent frameworks describe overlapping competencies with independent vocabulary.

biencoder_cosine.description: Bi-encoder sentence embeddings with cosine similarity.
biencoder_cosine.significance: Bi-encoder 0.562 < STS 0.688 (gap -0.126). Cross-attention finds deeper alignment than independent encoding. The relationship depends on cross-text reasoning — concept-level mapping between [accesses, articulate, capabilities] and [access, conducting, consumer] that bi-encoders structurally cannot capture.

jaccard_token.description: Token-level Jaccard similarity (intersection/union of lowercased word tokens).
jaccard_token.significance: Jaccard 12.5%. Shared tokens: [needs, skill]. DCWF-only: [accesses, articulate, capabilities, collection, emerging, integrate]. WIDAI-only: [access, conducting, consumer, data, defined, discovery].
